objects.name: Within Campaign Impact
objects.description: Impacts activity have within a campaign, e.g. how behaviour impacts perceived legitimacy of a campaign asset, reach of campaign content, e.t.c.

objects.name: External to Campaign Impact
objects.description: Impacts activity have external to a campaign, e.g. how behaviour impacts targeted individuals or institutions, geopolitics, or the information ecosystem.

objects.name: Online Techniques
objects.description: Techniques deployed as part of online Influence Operations by threat actors with the goal of achieving Tactical objectives.

objects.name: Scales Developed by non-DISARM Defenders
objects.description: Scales / Frameworks which have been developed by non-DISARM individuals or organisations defending against Influence Operations.

objects.name: Vulnerability Exploited
objects.description: Cognitive, Technical, or Social vulnerabilities which have impacted a potential incident.

objects.name: Actor Analysis
objects.description: Assessments analysts can make about actors behind an Influence Operation.

objects.name: Impact on Asset
objects.description: Impact of activity on assets.

objects.name: Increase Asset Legitimacy
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have made campaign assets appear more legitimate / trustworthy as sources of information.

objects.name: Persist in the Information Space
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have made campaign assets more likely to remain active within an information Space.

objects.name: Conceal Presence of Coordination
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have masked coordination of online assets.

objects.name: Conceal Digital Infrastructure
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have masked information about digital infrastructure used to conduct campaign activity.

objects.name: Impact on Publication
objects.description: Impact of activity on publications.

objects.name: Maximise Exposure to Publication
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have significantly increased exposure to a publication.

objects.name: Impact on Campaign
objects.description: Impact of activity on the campaign as a whole.

objects.name: Break Association with Content
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have masked the association between assets used and the content produced as part of an operation.

objects.name: Conceal Operational Activity
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have masked activity taken as part of an operation.

objects.name: Conceal Sponsorship of Activity
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have masked the individual or institution who has financed activity.

objects.name: Distance Individuals from Operation
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have masked the association between individuals associated with the operation and the operation itself.

objects.name: Negative Reputational Impact
objects.description: Types of negative reputational impact resulting from campaign activity.

objects.name: Target of Activity Defamed
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have led to defamation of a target.

objects.name: Target of Activity Undermined
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have undermined a target.

objects.name: Target of Activity Discredited
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have discredited a target.

objects.name: Target of Activity Polarised
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have increased polarisation of an individual or community.

objects.name: Positive Reputational Impact
objects.description: Types of positive reputational impact resulting from campaign activity.

objects.name: Target of Activity Reputation Increased
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have increased the reputation of a target.

objects.name: Target of Activity Reputation Defended
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have defended the reputation of a target against a negative narrative.

objects.name: Target of Activity Prestige Increased
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have increased the prestige of a target.

objects.name: Support Cultivated for Target of Activity
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have increased broad support for a target.

objects.name: Supporters of Target of Activity Energised
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have energised existing supporters of a target.

objects.name: Supporters Recruited for Target of Activity
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have recruited new explicit supporters for a target.

objects.name: Behaviour which is Target of Activity is Justified
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have justified behaviour of a target.

objects.name: Impact on Narrative
objects.description: Impacts that activity can have on a narrative.

objects.name: Activity Increases Perceived Legitimacy of Narrative
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have increased the perceived legitimacy a narrative.

objects.name: Activity Introduces Another Narrative which Competes with Target Narrative
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have produced a narrative which contrasts another narrative which previously existed.

objects.name: Activity Suppresses Reach of Narrative
objects.description: Activity has suppressed a narrative's ability to reach a given audience.

objects.name: Activity Increases Perceived Popularity of Narrative
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of increasing the perception of how popular a narrative appears to be.

objects.name: Activity Distracts from Narrative
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of drawing attention away from a specific narrative.

objects.name: Impact on Subject
objects.description: Impacts that activity can have on defenders.

objects.name: Activity Exhausts Subject's Resources
objects.description: Activity has resulted in the subject of an incident exhausting their available resources.

objects.name: Activity Causes Subject to Change Living Location
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject to relocate from their place of residence.

objects.name: Subject Dies
objects.description: The subject has died in connection with or as a result of activity.

objects.name: Financial Impact
objects.description: Types of financial impact resulting from campaign activity.

objects.name: Stocks of Target of Activity Negatively Impacted
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have negatively impacted the price of a target's stocks.

objects.name: Stocks of Target of Activity Positively Impacted
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have positively impacted the price of a target's stocks.

objects.name: Funds Raised for Target of Activity
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have led to funds being contributed to a target.

objects.name: Receive Funds from Target of Activity
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have led to funds being received from a target.

objects.name: Subject's Funding Negatively Impacted
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of reducing or eliminating sources of funding available to the subject.

objects.name: Geopolitical Impact
objects.description: Types of geopolitical impact resulting from campaign activity.

objects.name: Economic Geopolitical Advantage
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have an economic geopolitical advantage.

objects.name: Domestic Political Advantage
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have a domestic political advantage.

objects.name: Impact on Behaviour
objects.description: Types of impact on behaviours resulting from campaign activity.

objects.name: Decision to Act Influenced by Activity
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have contributed to the choice for another individual or institution to take an action.

objects.name: Activity Dissuades Action
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have contributed to the choice for another individual or institution to not take an action.

objects.name: Activity Thwarts Action
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have prevented another actor from succeeding in taking desired activity.

objects.name: Action Taken in Response to Activity
objects.description: An action has been taken in direct response to previous activity.

objects.name: Emotional Impact
objects.description: Types of impact activity can have on target's emotional state.

objects.name: Feeling of Admiration Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of admiration in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Adoration Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of adoration in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Aesthetic Appreciation Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of aesthetic appreciation in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Amusement Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of amusement in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Anger Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of anger in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Anxiety Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of anxiety in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Awe Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of awe in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Awkwardness Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of awkwardness in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Boredom Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of boredom in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Calmness Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of calmness in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Confusion Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of confusion in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Craving Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of craving in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Disgust Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of disgust in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Empathic Pain Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of empathic pain in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Entrancement Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of entrancement in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Excitement Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of excitement in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Fear Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of fear in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Horror Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of horror in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Interest Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of interest in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Joy Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of joy in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Nostalgia Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of nostalgia in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Relief Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of relief in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Romance Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of romance in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Sadness Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of sadness in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Satisfaction Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of satisfaction in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Sexual Desire Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of sexual desire in the subject.

objects.name: Feeling of Surprise Evoked in Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of evoking a feeling of surprise in the subject.

objects.name: Impact on Information Ecosystem
objects.description: Types of impact on the information ecosystem resulting from campaign activity.

objects.name: Spread Hate
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have increased the level of negative / hateful messaging for a target individual or community within an information ecosystem.

objects.name: Degrade Quality of Information Ecosystem
objects.description: Activity has been assessed to have degraded the quality of an information ecosystem (e.g. reduced the reliability of the average piece of information within the ecosystem).

objects.name: Activity Causes Loss of Existing Information
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing previously available information to be removed from an information ecosystem.

objects.name: Activity Causes Subject to Disengage
objects.description: This Category contains impacts related to the subject withdrawing from an activity, community, or cause as a result of the campaign.

objects.name: Subject Disengages from Information Ecosystem
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject to disengage from an information ecosystem.

objects.name: Subject Disengages from Community
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject to disengage from a community they were part of.

objects.name: Subject Disengages from Cause
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject to disengage from a cause or movement they were involved in.

objects.name: Subject Declines to Start Sharing Information
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject to decide against sharing information they would otherwise have shared.

objects.name: Subject Ceases Sharing Information
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject to stop sharing information they had previously been sharing.

objects.name: Subject Self-Censors when Sharing Information
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject to modify or limit the information they share when discussing a given topic area.

objects.name: Subject's Audience Disengages with Information Shared by Subject
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of causing the subject's audience to reduce or cease engagement with information shared by the subject.

objects.name: Impact on Subject's Freedom
objects.description: This Category contains impacts related to restrictions on the subject's freedoms as a result of the campaign.

objects.name: Activity Restricts Subject's Financial Freedom
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of limiting the subject's ability to access or manage their finances.

objects.name: Activity Restricts Subject's Freedom of Speech
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of limiting the subject's ability to express themselves freely.

objects.name: Activity Restricts Subject's Freedom of Movement
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of limiting the subject's ability to move freely.

objects.name: Activity Restricts Subject's Academic Freedom
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of limiting the subject's ability to conduct research or express academic views freely.

objects.name: Activity Restricts Subject's Journalistic Freedom
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of limiting the subject's ability to carry out journalistic work freely.

objects.name: Activity Restricts Subject's Access to Information
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of limiting the subject's ability to access information.

objects.name: Activity Restricts Legally Protected Freedom
objects.description: Activity has the assessed impact of limiting a freedom the subject is legally entitled to.

objects.name: Delivery Techniques
objects.description: Techniques which are associated with delivering material online.

objects.name: Collaborating Assets Seed and Ping a Target
objects.description: Collaborating assets pose as being unassociated, with one seeding campaign material, and another pinging the campaign target, soliciting engagement with the campaign material.

objects.name: Flood an Online Information Space
objects.description: An asset has flooded an online information space by publishing content there many times, or many collaborating assets have flooded an online information space by each concurrently posting a small amount of content, or both.

objects.name: Publish Material Unrelated to a Campaign Narrative
objects.description: An asset has published material which is unrelated to the campaign's goals or target narratives.

objects.name: Psychological Techniques
objects.description: Techniques using psychology to influence the behaviour of a target.

objects.name: Coercion by Proxy
objects.description:
[Coercion by Proxy](https://freedomhouse.org/report/special-report/2020/home-and-abroad-coercion-proxy-tool-transnational-repression) refers to attempts to control a target through harming or threatening harm to individuals or institutions that the target cares for.

Instead of attacking a target directly, an actor harms or threatens to harm people the target has a relationship with (family members, colleagues, employers, schools, religious or civic institutions) most often with the goal of coercing, silencing, or punishing the target by proxy.

Sometimes referred to as “collective punishment”, authoritarian governments operating within their own territory use Coercion by Proxy to target the relatives, associates or institutions connected to critics living or working abroad, using domestic repression as a means of controlling people the state cannot otherwise reach. [Human Rights Watch's global review of how governments repress nationals abroad](https://www.hrw.org/report/2024/02/22/we-will-find-you/global-look-how-governments-repress-nationals-abroad) lists collective punishment of relatives as one of the core recurring methods of transnational repression.

Coercion by Proxy as a technique is best understood seen through the lens of the chilling effect; the mere possibility of punishment, independent of whether it is ever carried out, is enough to make people restrict their own behaviour out of fear and uncertainty. This matters for Coercion by Proxy specifically because the technique's deterrent power doesn't depend on the original target being harmed at all. A credible threat, or even a single demonstrated case against someone else's family, can chill an entire network of potential sources, journalists or activists who simply witness it.

objects.name: US DOJ Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle Assessment
objects.description: In 2018, the US Department of Justice introduced the Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle (MFICC) as part of its [Cyber-Digital Task Force report](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf); a framework mapping the phases of a foreign influence operation in order to identify opportunities for detection and disruption at each stage, and can be seen as analogous to a Kill Chain model. While broader in scope, it has a particular emphasis on operations targeting US elections and democratic institutions.

objects.name: Research Campaign Cycle Phase
objects.description:
Activity has been reviewed against the [US DOJ's Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf) and assessed to be in the Research stage.

During the Research stage, the actor identifies the vulnerabilities (both social and technical) it intends to exploit. This involves collection and analysis activities carried out by government research institutes and intelligence services, with the objective of mapping existing social and political fissures within the target society and developing message themes designed to inflame them.

objects.name: Position Campaign Cycle Phase
objects.description:
Activity has been reviewed against the [US DOJ's Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf) and assessed to be in the Position stage.

During the Position stage, the actor establishes the infrastructure and online presence needed to conduct the campaign. Intelligence services, contractors, agents, and assets work to procure technical infrastructure, develop and grow networks of accounts and personas, and establish the credibility of those personas.

objects.name: Tasking Campaign Cycle Phase
objects.description:
Activity has been reviewed against the [US DOJ's Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf) and assessed to be in the Tasking stage.

During the Tasking stage, the actor translates the research and analytical work of the previous stage into operational direction. Drawing on the message themes and targeting analysis developed during Research, leadership and intelligence services assign specific tasks. This stage acts as the bridge between strategic intent and operational activity, converting an understanding of the target society's vulnerabilities into concrete instructions the activity that follows.

objects.name: Produce Campaign Cycle Phase
objects.description:
Activity has been reviewed against the [US DOJ's Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf) and assessed to be in the Produce stage.

During the Produce stage, the actor creates or obtains content. This can involve hacking to acquire genuine material such as the theft of emails from target organisations), or the composition of fabricated or forged content. Intelligence services, contractors, state media, and other assets can play a role here.

objects.name: Publish Campaign Cycle Phase
objects.description:
Activity has been reviewed against the [US DOJ's Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf) and assessed to be in the Publish stage.

During the Publish stage, the actor introduces content into the broader media environment while deliberately obscuring its true origin. This is achieved through publication via online proxies and personas, and dissemination through the networks established in the Position stage.

objects.name: Amplify Campaign Cycle Phase
objects.description:
Activity has been reviewed against the [US DOJ's Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf) and assessed to be in the Amplify stage.

During the Amplify stage, the actor attempts to saturate the information environment and generate genuine public confusion. Bots and ‘trolls’ can artificially inflate the reach and apparent legitimacy of content, while state media and contractors push overt messaging. Unwitting audiences can also contribute as actors at this stage, amplifying foreign narratives without any awareness that they are doing so (for instance by sharing content or attending rallies organised covertly by foreign actors).

objects.name: Calibrate Campaign Cycle Phase
objects.description:
Activity has been reviewed against the [US DOJ's Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle](https://web.archive.org/web/20240616065252/https://media-cldnry.s-nbcnews.com/image/upload/today/z_creative/Cyber-Digital%20Task%20Force%20Report.pdf) and assessed to be in the Calibrate stage.

During the Calibrate stage, the actor gathers feedback and adapts to countermeasures deployed against them during the campaign; leadership and intelligence services assess the outcome of the campaign and retool their approach if needed. This emphasises the “Cycle” element of the Malign Foreign Influence Campaign Cycle; a foreign influence operation is not a linear, one-time effort but an iterative process that evolves in response to what is working.

objects.name: Cognitive Vulnerability
objects.description: Types of cognitive vulnerabilities; Inherent weaknesses in unconscious thinking patterns that distort a person's judgment and decision-making, which can contribute to the propagation of information disorder.

objects.name: Illusory Truth Effect
objects.description: The Illusory Truth Effect refers to the phenomenon where [individuals who are repeatedly exposed to a piece of information are more likely to believe it is true information.](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S2352250X23001811).

objects.name: Technical Vulnerability
objects.description: Types of technical vulnerabilities; issues with technology, online platforms, or their features, which can contribute to the propagation of information disorder.

objects.name: Low Quality Non-English Language Moderation
objects.description: Western platforms have been documented to have [lower quality moderation when operating in non-English languages.](https://globalwitness.org/en/campaigns/digital-threats/how-big-tech-platforms-are-neglecting-their-non-english-language-users/) This can result in erroneous moderation action, or in continuing to host Assets or Content which goes against the platform's stated terms of service.

objects.name: Financial Vulnerability
objects.description: This Category contains vulnerabilities related to the subject's financial exposure or dependency.

objects.name: Structural Financial Dependence
objects.description: The subject is structurally dependent on a source of funding that can be withdrawn or leveraged against them.

objects.name: Social Vulnerability
objects.description: This Category contains vulnerabilities related to a community or societal situation.

objects.name: Subject Lacks Necessary Support Network
objects.description: The subject lacks the social, professional, or institutional support network needed to withstand, report, or seek redress for the campaign's activity.

objects.name: Information Vulnerability
objects.description: This Category contains vulnerabilities related to an information environment.

objects.name: Information Vacuum
objects.description: An absence or scarcity of credible information on a topic within an information environment. Actors can exploit a vacuum by filling it with their own narratives before authoritative information becomes available, and can deliberately create one by suppressing or degrading access to credible sources.

objects.name: Echo Chamber
objects.description: An environment in which participants predominantly encounter information and views that reinforce those they already hold, formed through social self-selection of like-minded sources and communities rather than algorithmic curation. Actors can exploit echo chambers to seed and amplify narratives that face little internal challenge and harden the community against outside correction.

objects.name: Participant Motive
objects.description: This category contains assessments analysts can make about the motivation behind an individual's participation in an influence operation.

objects.name: Ideologically Aligned Participant
objects.description: The individual who took action as part of an incident has been assessed to have done so because they were ideologically aligned with the goals of the campaign.

objects.name: Paid Participant
objects.description: The individual who took action as part of an incident has been assessed to have done so because they were paid for their participation.

objects.name: Emotionally Motivated Participant
objects.description: The individual who took action as part of an incident has been assessed to have done so because they were emotionally motivated (e.g. they acted out of anger, or fear).

objects.name: Participant Awareness
objects.description: This category contains assessments analysts can make about whether an individual was aware that they were contributing to an influence operation.

objects.name: Participant Unaware of Contribution to Campaign
objects.description: The individual who took action as part of an incident has been assessed to have been unaware that they were doing so.

objects.name: Participant Aware of Contribution to Campaign
objects.description: The individual who took action as part of an incident has been assessed to have been aware that they were doing so.

objects.name: Alignment with Actor Goals
objects.description: This Category contains Assessments of whether observed narratives or actions are aligned with, or opposed to, the goals of the campaign actor.

objects.name: Narrative Aligned with Actor Goals
objects.description: The narrative is assessed to align with the goals of the campaign actor.

objects.name: Narrative Opposed to Actor Goals
objects.description: The narrative is assessed to oppose or undermine the goals of the campaign actor.

objects.name: Action Aligned with Actor Goals
objects.description: The action taken by the observed participant is assessed to align with the goals of the campaign actor.

objects.name: Action Opposed to Actor Goals
objects.description: The action taken by the observed participant is assessed to oppose or undermine the goals of the campaign actor.

objects.name: Target Type
objects.description: This Category contains Assessments of whether an observed participant was the primary or a secondary target of the activity.

objects.name: Primary Target
objects.description: The participant is assessed to be the primary target of the observed behaviour.

objects.name: Secondary Target
objects.description: The participant is assessed to be a secondary target, i.e. an individual or institution intentionally acted upon in order to indirectly affect the primary target.

objects.name: Impact Intentionality
objects.description: This Category contains Assessments of whether an observed impact was intended or unintended by the campaign actor.

objects.name: Intended Impact
objects.description: The assessed impact is considered to be an intentional goal of the campaign actor's behaviour.

objects.name: Unintended Impact
objects.description: The assessed impact is considered to be an unintended consequence of the campaign actor's behaviour.

objects.name: DISARM Foundation
objects.description: DISARM is a framework designed for describing and understanding disinformation incidents.

objects.name: DISARM V2 Assessments Framework
objects.description: The DISARM V2 Assessments Framework standardizes the descriptions of assessments made by analysts which pertain to suspected adversary operations or campaigns.
